name: Germany
summary: Acute software/AI shortage plus tuition-free public MSc make Germany a strong study->Blue Card->fast-PR route for two engineers; no APS and Dhaka-decided visas help, but B1 German for citizenship, high deductions and rising anti-immigrant politics temper it.

links.title: Make it in Germany (official portal)

job-market.summary: Acute software/AI shortage; ~109k unfilled IT roles, 137k+ open software jobs, both engineers easily absorbed.
job-market.reasoning: Bitkom 2025 reports a shortage of ~109,000 IT specialists with 137,000+ open software roles and an average 7.7 months to fill a vacancy; 85% of firms report a shortage and 79% expect it to worsen. Demand easily absorbs two CS graduates, and software/AI/data roles top the bottleneck list, with English-speaking tech scenes in Berlin, Munich and Hamburg.
evidence: Bitkom 2025: ~109,000 IT specialists missing; 137,000+ open roles
Average IT vacancy takes 7.7 months to fill (Bitkom 2025)
85% of firms report a shortage; 79% expect it to worsen
Software devs, AI/ML, data scientists, cybersecurity top demand
Cloud skills gap could reach 200,000 by 2030 (Bitkom)

links.title: Bitkom: Germany still lacks more than 100,000 IT specialists (2025)
EU Blue Card Germany (Make it in Germany, official)

visa-access.summary: Decided in Dhaka, no APS and no New Delhi travel; but VFS-intake backlog and €11,904 blocked account weigh.
visa-access.reasoning: The German Embassy Dhaka handles and decides every student visa locally with no New Delhi travel, and crucially APS is NOT required for Bangladeshi applicants (unlike India/China/Vietnam). Friction remains: master applications are taken via VFS since 02.01.2025, a blocked account of €11,904 (€992/month) for 2026 is required, and the embassy admits appointment waits are unpredictable after applicant numbers rose exponentially.
evidence: No APS required for Bangladesh (Embassy Dhaka FAQ)
Embassy Dhaka decides every application locally; no New Delhi travel
Master-student visa intake via VFS Global since 02.01.2025
Blocked account €11,904/year (€992/month) for 2026
Embassy states wait times unpredictable amid exponential demand

links.title: FAQ - Student Visa (German Embassy Dhaka, official)
Information regarding Study Visa (German Embassy Dhaka, official)

citizenship.summary: Standard 5 years + dual citizenship allowed, but 3-year fast-track repealed 30 Oct 2025; B1 + test required.
citizenship.reasoning: The 2024 StAG reform keeps the 5-year naturalisation path and permits dual citizenship for Bangladeshis (no renunciation), but the 3-year 'turbo' route was repealed effective 30 October 2025. Applicants still need B1 German, the Leben-in-Deutschland test and proof of self-sufficiency, placing this at the ~5yr+dual anchor with a language bar.
evidence: Standard naturalisation 5 years; dual citizenship retained (StAG 2024)
3-year fast-track repealed, in force 30 Oct 2025 (Bundestag 8 Oct 2025)
Dual citizenship permitted for non-EU nationals incl. Bangladeshis
Requires B1 German + integration test + no reliance on benefits
3-year path survives only for spouses of German citizens (§9 StAG)

links.title: Law scrapping 3-year citizenship route in effect (IamExpat)
Naturalisation in Germany (BAMF, official)

post-study-work.summary: 18-month post-study job-search permit with unrestricted work, in-country switch to work visa or Blue Card.
post-study-work.reasoning: German-university graduates get an 18-month residence permit (§20) to seek qualified work, with the right to take ANY job during the search, then switch to a work permit or EU Blue Card from inside Germany. The 18 months starts at graduation; the spouse, who can hold their own student/graduate permit, works in parallel. Falls short of the 3-year open-work top anchor.
evidence: 18-month job-search residence permit for German graduates (§20)
Any employment allowed during the 18-month search
In-country switch to work permit / EU Blue Card
Clock starts at graduation, not permit-card issue date
Both partners as graduates can each hold their own PSW permit

links.title: Working in Germany After Graduation 2026 (Studying in Germany)
Jobs after studies (Make it in Germany, official)

spouse-family.summary: Both work freely in every phase; reuniting Blue Card spouse gets immediate unlimited work, no German needed.
spouse-family.reasoning: As two qualified engineers each can hold an independent student, job-search or work permit, so both work in every phase. BAMF confirms a spouse joining an EU Blue Card holder gets a residence title with NO German requirement and immediate, unlimited permission to work. The subsidiary-protection family-reunion suspension (Jul 2025) does not touch Blue Card or skilled-worker families.
evidence: Blue Card spouse: residence title without German + immediate unlimited work (BAMF)
Both partners can each hold their own student/work permit throughout
Family-reunion suspension (24 Jul 2025) exempts Blue Card holders
No dependant work ban during study phase for this couple
Sponsor income requirement modest for two skilled earners

links.title: The EU Blue Card (BAMF, official)
Suspension of family reunification - Blue Card exempt (Federal Foreign Office)

msc-study.summary: Abundant English-taught CS MSc, ~€0 tuition + ~€150-350 semester fee, student work rights, spouse accompanies.
msc-study.reasoning: Most public universities charge only a ~€150-350 semester contribution with large English-taught CS Master's catalogues (RWTH Aachen alone lists 60+; 46+ all-English CS programmes nationally) and DAAD funding. Students may work 120 full / 240 half days a year and the spouse can accompany. Exceptions: Baden-Württemberg charges €1,500/sem for non-EU students.
evidence: No tuition at most public universities; only ~€150-350 semester fee
46+ all-English CS Master's programmes; RWTH Aachen 60+
Student work allowance 120 full / 240 half days per year
DAAD funding and International Programmes database available
Exception: Baden-Württemberg €1,500/sem for non-EU students

links.title: DAAD International Programmes database (official)
English-Taught Programmes in Germany 2026 (GradGermany)

pr-pathway.summary: Blue Card settlement permit in 21 months (B1) or 27 (A1) with social contributions; study time can count.
pr-pathway.reasoning: BAMF confirms EU Blue Card holders qualify for a settlement permit (Niederlassungserlaubnis) after 21 months with B1 German or 27 months with A1, far faster than the standard 5 years. The 2026 Blue Card salary floor (~€45,934 for IT/shortage, €50,700 standard) is easily met by SW engineers, and graduate study time can count toward residence. Predictable and points-light.
evidence: Settlement permit at 21 months with B1 on Blue Card (BAMF)
27 months with A1 German on Blue Card (BAMF)
2026 Blue Card floor: ~€45,934 shortage/IT, €50,700 standard
Standard route 5 years; graduate study time may count
Requires ongoing pension contributions + integration test

links.title: The EU Blue Card (BAMF, official)
Settlement permit (Make it in Germany, official)

income-cost.summary: Strong gross SW pay (~€64-74k avg) but 35-40% deductions and high metro rents; two incomes still save.
income-cost.reasoning: Average software-engineer pay is ~€64,000-74,100 gross in 2026, but deductions of 35-40% leave only ~55-65% net (a €74,100 gross nets ~€45-50k), and Berlin/Munich rents are high. Two engineering incomes still allow meaningful savings, and AI/cloud specialisations push pay to the upper range, but tax and rent erode net relative to lower-tax peers.
evidence: Average SW gross ~€64,000; ~€74,100 in some 2026 datasets
Berlin median total comp ~€90,601; Munich ~€78,876
Deductions 35-40%: €74,100 gross nets ~€45-50k
High metro rents in Berlin/Munich erode savings
Two incomes + AI/cloud premium enable real savings

links.title: Software Engineer Salary in Germany 2026 (WBS Coding School)
Salary, taxes & social security (Make it in Germany, official)

healthcare.summary: Universal statutory insurance (GKV); ~€130-151/month student rate, high quality, strong welfare.
healthcare.reasoning: Mandatory statutory health insurance (GKV) covers residents comprehensively at high quality, with a discounted student rate of ~€130-151/month in 2026 (TK/AOK Bayern from €141.16) and same-quality access for workers. Welfare (e.g. Elterngeld parental allowance) is strong. Slightly below the universal-and-free top anchor because contributions are non-trivial.
evidence: Statutory health insurance (GKV) universal for residents
Student rate ~€130-151/month in 2026 (from €141.16 TK/AOK)
Student general contribution 10.22% vs 14.6% for employees
eGK health card issued within 2-3 weeks of enrolment
Strong parental allowance (Elterngeld) and welfare net

links.title: Public Health Insurance (GKV) for Students 2026 (student-insurance.com)
Health insurance (Make it in Germany, official)

culture-language.summary: English works in tech, but B1 German is mandatory for citizenship and daily officialdom outside hubs.
culture-language.reasoning: Tech workplaces in Berlin/Munich/Hamburg often operate in English, but German is required for bureaucracy, faster PR (B1 cuts the Blue Card settlement clock to 21 months) and naturalisation (B1 + Leben-in-Deutschland test), and for daily life outside major hubs. This local-language bar for officialdom and the couple's citizenship goal holds it at the mixed band.
evidence: English common in major-hub tech workplaces
B1 German required for naturalisation + integration test
B1 unlocks 21-month Blue Card settlement vs 27 (A1)
German needed for officialdom and daily life off-hub
High livability and openness in cities, lower in some regions

links.title: Learning German (Make it in Germany, official)
Naturalisation in Germany (BAMF, official)

safety-law.summary: WJP 2025: 6th of 143 globally (0.83), top-5 order & security; strong legal protections.
safety-law.reasoning: Germany ranks 6th of 143 in the WJP Rule of Law Index 2025 with an overall score of 0.83, and is 5th globally for order and security, with low violent crime and an established anti-discrimination framework. Top-tier rule of law and fair courts; tempered slightly by rising societal hostility toward migrants and Muslims.
evidence: WJP Rule of Law Index 2025: 6th of 143, score 0.83
5th globally for order & security (after Nordics)
Low violent crime relative to global peers
Established anti-discrimination legal framework
Rising societal hostility tempers the migrant experience

links.title: WJP Rule of Law Index 2025 - Germany
WJP Rule of Law Index 2025 Global Press Release

politics.summary: Stable institutions but CDU/SPD migration tightening, AfD 20.8% second; skilled-worker routes preserved.
politics.reasoning: Institutions are stable under the Merz CDU/CSU-SPD grand coalition (formed 6 May 2025) and skilled-worker/Blue Card routes are deliberately preserved, but migration policy is tightening (3-year citizenship repealed; family reunion suspended for subsidiary protection, though Blue Card holders are exempt). The AfD's 20.8% second-place finish signals a rising anti-immigrant trend.
evidence: CDU/CSU-SPD grand coalition under Merz from 6 May 2025
AfD 20.8%, second place, Feb 2025 federal election
Skilled-worker and EU Blue Card pathways preserved
3-year fast-track citizenship abolished (Oct 2025)
Family reunion suspension (Jul 2025) targets refugees, not Blue Card

links.title: Merz cabinet (Wikipedia, sourced)
Germany suspends family reunification for subsidiary protection (Library of Congress)

tax.summary: High burden: progressive income tax to 42% plus ~20% employee social contributions.
tax.reasoning: The 2026 income tax rises to 42% above €69,878 (45% above €277,825) on top of ~20% employee social contributions (9.3% pension, 7.3% health, etc.), pushing total deductions to ~35-40% of gross for SW salaries. The 2026 basic allowance rose to €12,348, but take-home for mid/high earners stays around 55-65%.
evidence: 42% marginal income tax from €69,878 (45% above €277,825), 2026
Employee social contributions ~20% of gross
Basic allowance €12,348 in 2026 (up from €12,096)
Total deductions ~35-40% of gross for SW salaries
Joint married allowance €24,696 helps a dual-earner couple

links.title: Germany: Taxing Wages 2026 (OECD, official)
Salary, taxes & social security (Make it in Germany, official)

muslim-diaspora.summary: ~5.5M Muslims, halal easy in cities, but small Bangladeshi community (~20k) and sharply rising Islamophobia.
muslim-diaspora.reasoning: Germany has ~5.5 million Muslims with halal food and worship easy in cities, but the Bangladeshi community is small (~16,000-20,000, with South Asian presence mainly Pakistani). CLAIM recorded 3,080 anti-Muslim incidents in 2024, a ~60% jump from 1,926 in 2023, and surveys find Muslims the least-accepted minority - a worsening sentiment trend amid the AfD's rise.
evidence: ~5.5 million Muslims in Germany; halal widely available in cities
Bangladeshi community small: ~16,000-20,000
CLAIM 2025: 3,080 anti-Muslim incidents in 2024, +60% vs 2023
Surveys: Muslims the least-accepted minority in Germany
~70% of victims were Muslim women; Berlin alone 644 cases

links.title: Germany Records Over 3,000 Anti-Muslim Incidents in 2024 (CLAIM data)
EU FRA anti-Muslim hatred database (official)
